title: Context is not optional
body:
## Summary
The description if the `build` command states that `CONTEXT` is optional:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/2e0ee6ed50aeae7c0219205cbbc6150ccc70448b/cmd/podman/images/build.go#L51

However one can easily prove that this is not true:

## Steps to reproduce
```bash
mkdir example
cd example
touch Containerfile
podman build
```

### Expected result
Podman should discover the Containerfile in the current directory and use current directory as context.

### Actual result
Podman throws error `Error: no context directory and no Containerfile specified`

## Further notes
- Also the [manual states](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-build.1.html) that context is optional 
- I am not sure what the intended behavior is.
  - Should podman discover the context/containerfile automatically?
  - or are the docs wrong and context is only optional if a containerfile is specified with `-f`?

comments.body:
This is a good first issue, I am leaving a short summary here if someone intends to pick it as a starting issue :)

Summary:
So buildah sets cwd as the contextdir https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/pkg/cli/build.go#L155 if context dir is empty while podman throws error https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/cmd/podman/images/build.go#L277

So podman just needs to set context as cwd instead of throwing this error, and maybe retrofit some tests.

Well you could argue that `podman build` is matching the behaviour of `docker build`

Man page shows it as options `podman build --help` shows it as required.

But I am fine with making it match buildah. I don't see this as a breaking change.

As a user I think its fine either way (either update docs to match docker behavior, or update code to match docs & buildah)

Updating the docs and leave the code to match docker behavior probably has slightly less potential for breaking things (and tests don't need to be changed).

I more or less stumbled over the problem. I initially ran it with context and only after reading the docs thought "ah nice I can omit the context", only to scratch my head shortly afterwards what the heck I am doing wrong.
Then I guess updating docs should be enough for this, since `docker compat` is more important afaics.

SGTM
@flouthoc Could you open a PR?
